run_metadata.coverage_summary: Swept all nine Tier-1 government portals via web search (AusTender, buy.nsw/NSW eTendering, Buying for Victoria, QTenders/supply.qld, Tenders WA, SA Tenders and Contracts, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders). Sampled Tier-2 (local government via VendorPanel public tenders, universities, NSW/QLD Local Health District procurement) and ran the mandatory Tier-3 corporate check (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network). Captured six buyer-originated opportunities not seen in prior runs: five Commonwealth AusTender approaches to market (a DCCEEW GEMS verification-testing laboratory panel, a DCCEEW education-and-training EOI, an Australian Digital Health Agency pre-employment screening RFT, a Museum of Australian Democracy exhibition-design RFT, and an Office of Parliamentary Counsel printing RFT) plus one Queensland local-government RFQ (Douglas Shire Council, run via VendorPanel). Two genuinely new buyer sources were recorded (Douglas Shire Council on VendorPanel, and Townsville Hospital and Health Service's own procurement page). As in every recent run, direct page retrieval was blocked: WebFetch returned HTTP 403 to every external URL this run, so all detail is limited to publicly-searchable headline/snippet level and no specification documents could be opened; no publicly-visible Australian corporate RFx was retrievable without a supplier login.
gaps: Direct page retrieval (WebFetch) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden for every external site this run, including tenders.gov.au ATM detail pages and VendorPanel listing pages. Tender specification documents, contact details, evaluation criteria and values could not be extracted; all findings rely on public search snippets and are flagged as pending a detail pass.

SA Tenders and Contracts, Tenders Tasmania and NT QTOL: no new open opportunity distinct from prior runs surfaced above the search-snippet threshold this run; their landing/search endpoints were reachable but individual current-tender listings were not retrievable via WebFetch (403). Deferred deeper listing extraction to a future run.

Tier-3 corporate opportunities (SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery) remain registration/login-gated; no publicly-visible Australian corporate RFx was retrievable without a supplier account this run. The portal is recorded in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration; no corporate tenders were fabricated.
Invitation-only / panel-restricted RFx (e.g. management-consultancy RFPs sent to shortlisted firms) are out of scope and were not sought.

Museum of Australian Democracy exhibition-design RFT (MoAD-ATM-7493) closes 20 July 2026 (today, Sydney time); it is captured as closing-soon and may already be closed by ingestion time. Its industry briefing/site inspection was held 24 June 2026 and has passed.

Douglas Shire Council RFQ closing date (approx. early August 2026) was visible only in an aggregator/search snippet and was not verified against the buyer's own VendorPanel detail page (403); recorded as approximate and pending verification.

Many listings returned by the search index had already-passed closing dates or were captured in prior runs (the public search index continues to lag by roughly 1-2 weeks) and were excluded to avoid re-recording stale or duplicate entries.
recommended_seed_additions: Douglas Shire Council (QLD) — buyer-originated council procurement run through VendorPanel Marketplace (pages.vendorpanel.com/workwithdouglasshire); worth monitoring weekly for far-north-Queensland local-government RFQs/RFTs.

Townsville Hospital and Health Service — buyer-originated Queensland public-health procurement page (townsville.health.qld.gov.au/about-us/procurement-and-tenders), a Hospital and Health Service publishing outside the core QTenders row view; use as a seed to find sibling QLD HHS procurement pages.

tenders.title: GEMS Act verification testing laboratory services (panel)
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)
tenders.description: DCCEEW is seeking accredited testing laboratories to undertake verification testing of products regulated under the Greenhouse and Energy Minimum Standards Act 2012 (GEMS Act), to confirm compliance with mandatory regulatory requirements and verify manufacturers' energy-efficiency and performance claims. The procurement is to establish a panel arrangement.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier(s) must provide laboratory verification testing of GEMS-regulated products so the department can check that the products meet mandatory minimum energy-performance standards and that suppliers' efficiency/performance claims are accurate. Delivery is national (and where required overseas) under a multi-supplier panel.
key_deliverables: Verification testing of GEMS-regulated products against the applicable standards
Compliance/verification test reports supporting regulatory enforcement
Testing capacity across product categories under a panel arrangement
tenders.scope_of_work: Testing laboratories with appropriate accreditation undertake verification testing of products regulated under the GEMS Act 2012; testing is used to verify compliance with mandatory regulatory requirements and to confirm the accuracy of energy-efficiency and performance claims made by manufacturers and suppliers. Delivery locations span ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT, TAS and overseas.
requirements: Appropriate testing-laboratory accreditation (as specified in the tender documents)
tenders.eligibility: Open approach to market to establish a panel arrangement
mandatory_conditions: Laboratory must hold appropriate accreditation to perform GEMS verification testing
tenders.delivery_location: National (ACT, NSW, VIC, SA, WA, QLD, NT, TAS) and overseas
tenders.reasoning: Found via AusTender current-ATM web search; buyer-originated Commonwealth notice published on AusTender by DCCEEW. Headline and scope taken from public search snippet of the ATM detail page.
tenders.notes: Panel arrangement. Deliverables/scope summarised from a public search snippet of the ATM detail page; contact, value, contract term, evaluation criteria and documents were not retrievable because WebFetch returned HTTP 403. Pending a detail pass against the notice's own documents.

tenders.title: Expression of Interest — Education and Training Services
tenders.buyer: Department of Climate Change, Energy, the Environment and Water (DCCEEW)
tenders.description: DCCEEW has published an Expression of Interest in the Education and Training Services category on AusTender.
tenders.reasoning: Found via AusTender current-ATM web search; buyer-originated Commonwealth EOI published on AusTender by DCCEEW, published 13 July 2026 (within the last 7 days).
tenders.notes: Headline only — the EOI's category, publish date (13 Jul 2026) and closing date (17 Aug 2026) were visible in the AusTender ATM index snippet, but the detailed scope, deliverables, contact and documents were not retrievable (WebFetch 403). Pending a detail pass.

tenders.title: Pre-employment Screening Services
tenders.description: The Australian Digital Health Agency is undertaking an open approach to market to establish a new contractual arrangement for pre-employment screening services that support recruitment, onboarding and personnel-security activities. The services form part of the Agency's personnel security framework.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier must provide pre-employment screening services supporting workforce integrity, personnel security and organisational risk management — including screening to support recruitment, onboarding and ongoing personnel-security activities across the Agency's workforce.
key_deliverables: Pre-employment screening / background checks supporting recruitment and onboarding
Screening services aligned to the Agency's personnel security framework
tenders.scope_of_work: The services form part of the Agency's personnel security framework and support recruitment and workforce engagement activities; the Agency currently engages an external provider and is going to open market to establish a new contractual arrangement for the provision of pre-employment screening services that support workforce integrity, personnel security and organisational risk management objectives.
tenders.reasoning: Found via AusTender web search for the Australian Digital Health Agency; buyer-originated Commonwealth RFT published on AusTender. Description drawn from the public search snippet of the ATM detail page.
tenders.notes: Closing date (29 Jul 2026, 5:00pm ACT) from the ACT/AusTender snippet; publish date, value, contact and documents not retrievable (WebFetch 403). Pending a detail pass.

tenders.title: 2D and 3D Exhibition Design Services — 'First Nations Peoples and Australian Democracy' exhibition
tenders.buyer: Museum of Australian Democracy at Old Parliament House (MoAD)
tenders.description: The Museum of Australian Democracy at Old Parliament House is seeking 2D and 3D exhibition design services for a new exhibition, 'First Nations Peoples and Australian Democracy', which explores First Nations governance, law and society and centres First Nations perspectives, developed with First Nations curators and advisors.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier must provide 2D and 3D exhibition design for the 'First Nations Peoples and Australian Democracy' exhibition — translating a curatorial narrative (drawing on artworks, archival materials and audio-visual media, and developed with First Nations curators and advisors) into a cohesive designed exhibition space at the Museum of Australian Democracy.
key_deliverables: 3D exhibition design (spatial/environmental design and build documentation)
Design that presents a cohesive narrative of First Nations experiences of democracy from pre-contact to today
tenders.scope_of_work: The exhibition explores First Nations governance, law and society, highlighting Indigenous practice of democracy; guided by Yindymarra it centres First Nations perspectives of time and place through the 'Everywhen', linking past, present and future, drawing on artworks, archival materials and audio-visual media, developed with First Nations curators and advisors.
tenders.briefing: Industry briefing and site inspection held Wednesday 24 June 2026 at the Museum of Australian Democracy at Old Parliament House (now passed); registration to attend was via email to stephanie.scroope@moadoph.gov.au by 5pm 23 June 2026.
tenders.delivery_location: Museum of Australian Democracy at Old Parliament House, Canberra ACT
tenders.reasoning: Found via AusTender web search for Museum of Australian Democracy exhibition design; buyer-originated Commonwealth RFT published on AusTender by MoAD. Scope, briefing and contact taken from the public search snippet of the ATM detail page.
tenders.notes: Closes 20 Jul 2026 (today, Sydney time) — captured as closing-soon and may already be closed by ingestion time. Closing time listed as 3:00pm ACT per the AusTender exhibition-design listing. Full documents and evaluation criteria not retrievable (WebFetch 403). Contact email surfaced in the briefing-registration snippet.

tenders.title: Printing services
tenders.description: The Office of Parliamentary Counsel has published an approach to market for printing services on AusTender.
tenders.reasoning: Found via AusTender current-ATM web search; buyer-originated Commonwealth notice published on AusTender by the Office of Parliamentary Counsel.
tenders.notes: Headline only. Publish date (30 Jun 2026) and closing date (29 Jul 2026) from the AusTender index snippet; scope, value, contact and documents not retrievable (WebFetch 403). Pending a detail pass.

tenders.title: Procurement Advisory and Tender Management Services (RFQ)
tenders.description: Douglas Shire Council invites quotations from suitably qualified and experienced respondents to provide procurement advisory and tender management services to support the delivery of the Council's major capital projects.
tenders.deliverables_summary: The supplier must provide procurement advisory and tender management services supporting delivery of Douglas Shire Council's major capital projects — advising on and managing the Council's procurement and tendering processes for those projects.
key_deliverables: Procurement advisory services for major capital projects
requirements: Suitably qualified and experienced in procurement advisory / tender management
tenders.eligibility: Open to suitably qualified and experienced respondents
tenders.reasoning: Found via web search of VendorPanel public tenders for Douglas Shire Council; buyer-originated local-government RFQ posted on the Council's chosen e-sourcing platform (VendorPanel).
tenders.notes: Closing date (~4 Aug 2026) seen only in an aggregator/search snippet and not verified against the buyer's own VendorPanel detail page (403); treat as approximate/pending verification. Full scope, contact and documents not retrievable.

discovered_sources.source_name: Douglas Shire Council — Tenders & Procurement (VendorPanel Marketplace)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Web search for VendorPanel public council tenders surfaced Douglas Shire Council's RFQ for procurement advisory / tender management services; the Council uses VendorPanel Marketplace (pages.vendorpanel.com/workwithdouglasshire) to invite quotes.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated far-north-Queensland local-government channel; a genuinely new buyer not previously recorded. Public tenders visible; quoting requires VendorPanel registration.

discovered_sources.source_name: Townsville Hospital and Health Service — Procurement and tenders
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Web search for NSW/QLD Local Health District procurement surfaced Townsville Hospital and Health Service's own procurement-and-tenders page, distinct from the core QTenders row view.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated Queensland public-health procurement page (a Hospital and Health Service). Use as a seed to find sibling QLD HHS procurement pages (e.g. Metro North, Cairns and Hinterland).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical Tier-1 seed; queried this run for recently-published ATMs.
discovered_sources.notes: Primary Commonwealth procurement portal; five opportunities captured this run. Detail pages returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch — only the search-index snippets were usable.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical Tier-2 local-government seed; queried this run for open council RFQs/RFTs.
discovered_sources.notes: Aggregated public-tenders board across many Australian councils; each council listing links to that buyer's own VendorPanel workspace. Used to surface the Douglas Shire Council RFQ.

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Business Network Discovery (SAP Ariba Discovery)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 corporate check; buyer-posting board on SAP Business Network.
discovered_sources.notes: Largest single slice of corporate opportunity, but full RFx detail requires a free supplier account; no publicly-visible Australian corporate RFx was retrievable without login this run. Portal recorded per policy; no corporate tenders fabricated.
